historical_significance: Oldest and best-known Jain temple in Delhi. Located opposite Red Fort at intersection of Netaji Subhas Marg and Chandni Chowk. Built 1656 according to Jain scholar Balbhadra Jain's compendium of Digambar Jain shrines in India. Shah Jahan invited several Agrawal Jain merchants to come and settle in city and granted them land south of Chandani Chauk around Dariba Gali permitting them to build temporary structure to house Jain temple. Agrawal Jain community acquired three marble idols installed by Jivaraj Papriwal under supervision of Bhattaraka Jinachandra in Samvat 1548 (1491 CE) for temple. Main icon that of Tirthankara Parshva. Temple underwent many alterations and additions in past and was enlarged in early 19th century. Red sandstone temple. Main entrance has high archway with golden inscription of Jain mantra. Temple complex consists of three levels with main sanctum on ground floor, library and museum on first floor, and hospital for birds on second floor.

Oldest Jain Temple in Hastinapur located in Meerut district, Uttar Pradesh. Main temple built 1801 under auspices of Raja Harsukh Rai who was imperial treasurer of Emperor Shah Alam II. Principal deity in main temple 16th Jain Tirthankar Shri Shantinath in Padmasana posture. 40 acre temple complex encloses centrally located Mukhya Shikhara surrounded by group of Jain temples dedicated to various tirthankaras, mostly built in late 20th century. Hastinapur capital city of Pandavas and Kauravas at time of Mahabharata. Main temple has white colored idol of Bhagwan Shantinath in Padmasana posture 1½ foot in height. Idol reverenced in V.S. 1548 by Bhattaraka Jin Chandra for Jeevraj Papariwal.

alternative_names: Shri Vimalnath Digambar Jain Atishay Kshetra
canonical_or_religious_significance: In Jain tradition, birthplace of 13th Tirthankar Lord Vimal Nath; Holy Land where four Kalyanakas (chayavan, birth, diksa and omniscience) of Tirthankar Vimalnath occurred
historical_significance: Located in Kampil town in Kayamganj tehsil of Farrukhabad district, Uttar Pradesh. Ancient town of Kampil today almost village used to be capital of Panchal kingdom during ancient times. River Ganges used to flow past this place. Town mentioned in Puranas, Jatakas, Mahabharat and other ancient epics of India. In Jain tradition, birthplace of 13th Tirthankar Lord Vimal Nath and Mahasati Draupadi and Guru Dronacharya of Mahabharata. Around 1800 years old temple committed to Bhagwan Vimalnatha 13th Tirthankara of Jainism. Temple has around 60 cm high dark colored symbol of Bhagawan Vimalanatha in Padmasana Posture. Idol around 2600 years old. Said icon recovered from Ganga river. Ancient temple of V.S. 492 constructed on 10 feet high platform about 1500 years old. Bhagwan Vimalnath's idol 2 feet high in cross legged seating posture principal deity black in color supposed to be more than 2500 years old.

alternative_names: Shri Digamber Jain Siddha Kshetra Aharji
historical_significance: Located in Rajasthan. Very ancient holy place important from historical and archaeological aspects. Jain idols recovered from land and ancient remains of Jain temples present in huge quantity. Inscriptions on rocks mention time of Vikram Samvat 1100-1500. Main temple of Shri 1008 Shantinath Bhagwan. Statues of Lord Kunthunath and Lord Arhannath on both sides of statue of Lord Shantinath. 18 feet high statue of Lord Shantinath very beautiful. Around main temple Chaubisi of three yugas (present, past and future of 24 Tirthankaras). Main temple built in 12th century and idol of Mulnayak Bhagwan Shantinath installed in Vikram Samvat 1237.

canonical_or_religious_significance: In Jain tradition, Atishaya Kshetra (place where miracles happen)
historical_significance: Digambar Jain temple dedicated to Chandraprabhu located in hill city Tijara in Khairthal-Tijara district of Rajasthan. 55 kilometres from Alwar and 110 kilometres from Delhi. Established 1956 CE by local Jain merchant after recovering idol of Digambar Chandraprabhu 8th Tirthankara of Jains during excavation. Subsequent discovery of other idols in same region reinforced belief that place was once Dehra meaning place of worship for Jains. Temple reflects blend of Jain and Islamic architectural styles. Exterior adorned with intricate carvings and sculptures depicting stories from Jain mythology and life of Tirthankaras crafted using red sandstone and white marble. Digambar Chandraprabhu's white stone idol about 15 inches tall installed in rectangular temple. Temple hall spacious enough to accommodate over 2000 people. Both idols with others installed in rectangular temple decorated with pinnacles. Temple walls have elaborate carvings paintings and intricate glasswork depicting various scenes. Township in name of Lord Chandraprabha as Chandralok City spread in more than 100 acre. In devotional tradition, site believed to have miraculous properties.

Mound located at Mathura in Uttar Pradesh. Name derived from modern temple of Hindu goddess Kankali. Famous Jain stupa excavated here in 1890-91 by Alois Anton Führer. Mound almost rectangular in shape 500 feet long by 350 feet broad. Brought forth many treasures of Jain art. Archaeological findings testifies existence of two Jain temples and stupas. Numerous Jain sculptures, Ayagapattas (tablet of homage), pillars, crossbeams and lintels found during archaeological excavations. Only site that proved Jains too built stupas. Archaeologists also found ruins of two Jain temples one belonging to Svetambar sect and other to Digambar sect. Site yielding artifacts dating back 2500 years or more. Over 110 ancient inscriptions found. Inscriptions from Kankali Tila presented as definitive proof that Jainism independent ancient religion. Indicate presence of Jain temples in Mathura even 200 years before Lord Buddha. Inscriptions dating from 150 BCE to 1023 CE.

Group of 31 Jain temples located at Deogarh in Lalitpur district, Uttar Pradesh built around 8th to 17th century CE. Protected by Archaeological Survey of India through Northern Circle Office located in Lucknow. ASI maintains archaeological museum at Deogarh site noted for treasured archaeological sculptures. Fort temples dominated by Jain temples in eastern part of hill fort. Jain images mostly of iconographic and stylistic variety. Complex consists of 31 Jain temples housing around 2000 sculptures which are largest such collection in world. Jain temples have large number of panels depicting scenes from Jain mythology, tirthankara images and votive tablets. Pillars carved with thousand Jain figures. Idol of Bhagawan Shantinatha nearly 12 feet 6 inches tall and light red in color seated in Padmasana posture. Name Deogarh said to have originated from gods themselves dating back to 13th century. Site notable for many stone inscriptions from 8th to 15th century including unique inscription in eight languages.

Located in Rishabhdeo town of Udaipur District of Rajasthan. Temple considered important pilgrimage center by both Digambara and Śvētāmbara sect of Jainism. Ancient temple masterpiece of architecture. Temple of first Tirthankar of Jainism Lord Rishabhnath. Temple repaired in 14th and 15th centuries and oldest inscription found on temple is that of 1424 AD. Altar of temple built in 14th century. In Jain tradition, believed that Jains consider image belongs to time of Rama and Sita. Has 52 Jinalayas and 1100 pillars and also idols of Hindu deities like Durga Maa, Shiva and Lord Krishna. Main temple very attractive magnificent and artistic having 52 beautiful spires. According to Jain opinion as symbol of Nandishwar Dweep's 52 temples this temple constructed having 52 spires. Temple constructed scientifically according to art of architecture. Just before courtyard and in front of main temple idol of Marudevi Mata mother of Lord Rikhabdev seated on marble elephant. In tradition, said idol reverenced by Acharya Vidyanand in 8th-9th century. Ancient petrography also found proving Kshetra renovated time to time. Murti of Lord made of black stone about 3.5 feet high. In tradition, miraculous idol of Bhagwan Rishabh Dev brought from village Khoonadary which was prosperous city in ancient time.

Group of seven Śvetāmbara Jain temples inside Jaisalmer Fort in state of Rajasthan. Jaisalmer Fort UNESCO World Heritage Site as part of Hill Forts of Rajasthan. Fort has complex of seven Jain temples built by yellow sandstone during 12-16th century. Chandraprabha temple built 1509 CE. Askaran Chopra of Merta built huge temple dedicated to Sambhavanatha housing more than 600 idols and Gyan Bhandar (grand library) with oldest hand written books scripted on Bhojpatra and Tadputra. About 1000 old manuscripts written on palm leaf with painted wooden covers. Manuscripts date back to 12th century. Library also contains paintings astrological charts and copy of dronacharya's Oghaniryaktivritti. Chopra Panchaji built Ashtapadh temple inside fort. Temples well known for intricate carvings attracting many tourists and religious devotees.

canonical_or_religious_significance: In Jain tradition, Nirvana Sthana of Mahavira; place where 24th Tirthankara Bhagwan Mahavir attained Moksha (liberation) in 527 BCE
historical_significance: Sacred Jain temple dedicated to Bhagwan Mahavir 24th Tirthankar of Jain religion located at Pawapuri near Nalanda in Bihar. Temple has great significance as in Jain belief it marks place where Mahavir attained Moksha (liberation) and his final rites performed. Believed Mahavir gave final sermon here in 527 BCE and attained Nirvana. Uniquely located in middle of serene lotus-filled tank symbolizing purity and peace. White marble architecture enhances divine aura making it enchanting sight for visitors. Pilgrims from across country visit Jal Mandir to pay tribute to Mahavir and experience tranquil atmosphere. Temple integral part of Jain Tirth circuit attracting spiritual seekers and history enthusiasts alike. Temple's beauty combined with historical significance makes it one of most prestigious Jain temples in India. Temple built in middle of tank connected to land by small bridge. In Jain tradition, temple originally built by King Nandivardhan, elder brother of Mahavira.

Located in Pilani in Jhunjhunu district of Rajasthan. Pilani small town in Shekhawati region of Rajasthan. Important Jain temple in region. Temple features traditional Shekhawati architecture with intricate carvings and paintings.

Located in Moujmabad near Jaipur Rajasthan. Huge temple with 121 pillars. Approximately 225 idols made of stone and various metals installed. Area known for ancient art and sculptures. Idols include Prabhu Adinath Prabhu Ajitanath and other Tirthankaras.

Located within Kalinjar Fort in Banda district of Uttar Pradesh. Fort home to number of Hindu and Jain temples. Fort has been historically significant place from ancient times. Mentioned in Puranas and Mahabharata. Fort contains many ancient structures and temples with intricate carvings. Important archaeological site.

Located in Jhunjhunu Rajasthan. Important Jain temple in Shekhawati region. Features intricate domes spires and pillars showcasing skill of artisans. Elements carefully crafted with attention to detail adding to beauty and holiness. Inner sanctum called garbhagriha holds main deity or symbol of Jain principles. Important religious site for Jain community in region.
